failedAssertions.reason: Mathematical answer incorrect (expected x=7, got x=3). Verification status is 'partial' instead of 'passed'. Root cause: Backend running in mock mode without real LLM API key (OPENROUTER_API_KEY='your_key_here'). Mock mode returns generic placeholder responses instead of real mathematical reasoning.

Derivative answer incorrect (expected 3x²+2, got 'The result is 3'). Verification status is 'partial' instead of 'passed'. Root cause: Backend running in mock mode without real LLM API key. Mock mode returns generic placeholder responses instead of real calculus computations.

Refinement answers differ between actions for the same problem (default: 'The result is 3', simpler: 'x = 3', detailed: 'The result is 3'). Format inconsistencies between responses. Root cause: Backend running in mock mode without real LLM API key. Mock mode returns inconsistent placeholder responses instead of coherent refinement variations.

details.testingApproach: Two concurrent flow validator subagents tested 10 E2E assertions against the backend API running on localhost:3000. Tests covered text-only algebra/calculus, screenshot processing, refinement flows, error recovery, low-confidence handling, verification degradation, schema validation, and concurrent request handling.
details.rootCauseAnalysis: All 3 failures are attributable to the backend running in mock mode. The OPENROUTER_API_KEY environment variable is set to 'your_key_here' instead of a real API key. This causes the backend to return generic placeholder responses with incorrect mathematical answers. The API infrastructure, request/response handling, screenshot processing, refinement logic, and verification degradation all work correctly. Only the LLM integration produces incorrect mathematical content due to the missing valid API key.
whatWorks: Screenshot-based question processing (VAL-E2E-003) - Image extraction and LaTeX parsing work correctly
Screenshot refinement with context preservation (VAL-E2E-005) - Stateless design maintains context across requests
Error recovery flow (VAL-E2E-006) - Server remains healthy after malformed requests
Low-confidence image with text fallback (VAL-E2E-007) - System correctly uses clear text over poor image
Verification failure graceful degradation (VAL-E2E-008) - Responses delivered with 'partial' verification
Schema validation (VAL-E2E-009) - All required fields present in responses
Concurrent request handling (VAL-E2E-010) - 5 parallel requests handled correctly
details.recommendation: Configure a valid OPENROUTER_API_KEY environment variable and re-run the validation. The backend implementation is correct; only the LLM integration needs a real API key for mathematical correctness.
